newsletter.title: The slow-news edition
newsletter.blurb: A short letter from the homes — new openings, neighborhood notes, and direct-only rates. About once a month.

home.metaDescription: Homes Not Rooms. Premium serviced apartments in New Cairo and Golden Gates — book direct and save vs OTAs.
home.heroSubline: Premium stays across New Cairo and Golden Gates — designed to feel lived-in, run like a hotel, priced lower when you book direct.

destinations.title: Two cities, seven addresses.
destinations.subtitle: From the residential hush of Lotus to the airport-side calm of Golden Gates — pick the corner of Cairo that fits the trip.

featured.title: Open this week.
featured.subtitle: A handful of homes our team would put their friends in. Direct rates undercut the platforms, and the savings line is our promise.

pillars.title: Homes that feel lived-in.
pillars.body: Real kitchens, generous beds, books you'd actually read. None of the hotel emptiness.

pillars.title: Operations that feel like a hotel.
pillars.body: A real concierge. Replies in minutes, not hours. Airport pickup, doctor on call, dinner at 11 p.m.

pillars.title: Tech that gets out of your way.
pillars.body: Smart check-in. Mobile key. Digital guidebook in your pocket. No keys, no clipboards, no awkward 3 a.m. handovers.

pillars.title: Local hands, global standards.
pillars.body: Egyptian hospitality, run on the playbook of the best brands in the world. إكرام الضيف — translated into operations.

tech.title: Quietly competent.
tech.subtitle: Built so that 95% of the stay never needs us — and so that the 5% that does feels effortless.

capabilities.title: Smart check-in
capabilities.body: Mobile key delivered before you land. Backup keypad code in case your phone's flat.

capabilities.title: In-stay support
capabilities.body: A real person in your time zone, on WhatsApp. Most messages answered in under fifteen minutes.

capabilities.title: Digital guidebook
capabilities.body: Wi-Fi password, neighborhood notes, the breakfast cart we send guests to — all in your pocket.

testimonials.title: The kind of reviews you write a friend.

quotes.text: Smart lock worked first try. The team replied to a 2 a.m. message in fifteen minutes. Booked direct twice since.
quotes.name: Sarah K.

quotes.text: Saved real money booking direct vs the platforms. Same home, lower rate, better service.
quotes.name: Daniel S.

quotes.text: I came for two weeks and stayed seven. The Nomads floor changed how I work from Cairo.
quotes.name: Hala R.

quotes.text: Booking, key, kitchen, coffee — every step better than the hotel chains we usually use for company travel.
quotes.name: Ahmed F.

closing.title: When you book direct,
closing.titleAccent: the home gets to know you.
closing.subtitle: Lower rate. Real concierge. A relationship that doesn't end at checkout.

faq.title: Egypt-specific things worth knowing.
faq.subtitle: Eight questions guests ask most often. Direct answers, no marketing speak.

items.q: Can I stay with my partner?
items.a: Egyptian law asks for a marriage certificate when two Arab passport holders share a room. If both your passports are non-Arab, no certificate is needed. We'll explain the specifics warmly when you book — there's no surprise at check-in.
Same-gender visitors are welcome. Mixed-gender visitors are welcome only when they're first- or second-degree relatives. For other meetings, the lobby and the cafés a few minutes away are perfect.

items.q: What payment methods do you accept?
items.a: Local Meeza cards, all international cards, and bank transfers. We accept payment in EGP and USD. Booking direct skips the platform spread — you pay less for the same home.

Yes. Weekly and monthly discounts are applied automatically at booking — up to 22% off for stays of 30 nights or more. Our Nomads project at Golden Gates is built specifically for guests staying a month or longer.
Per home — some welcome pets, some don't. The detail is on each home page, and our concierge will sort the deposit and any small print before you arrive.

items.q: Can the team arrange airport pickup?
items.a: Yes — black cars, fixed pricing, English-speaking drivers, from Cairo, Alexandria, or Hurghada airports. Set it up at booking or any time after.

items.q: What's the Wi-Fi like for remote work?
items.a: Fast fiber, 100+ Mbps, in every home. Most homes have a dedicated workspace; our Nomads project includes a quiet co-working floor on the ground level.
A mobile key arrives in your phone before you land. There's a backup keypad code so you're never stuck. Land at 3 a.m., walk into a quiet, ready home — no front desk, no waiting.

about.metaDescription: The team, the philosophy, and the homes behind Travelholic.
about.heroSubline: We started Travelholic to make Cairo feel like home for the people we host. Five years on, that's still the only KPI that matters.

founder.name: Rashad Reda
founder.quote: We didn't set out to build a serviced-apartment company. We set out to host the way Egyptians host — and the company is what came out the other side. Every home we open, we open with the same question: would I send my mother here?

items.title: The guest comes first — even when no one is watching.
items.body: إكرام الضيف is the principle that the guest's comfort is your responsibility, not your performance. We hire for it, train for it, and fire for the absence of it.

items.title: Direct over distribution.
items.body: Platforms are a fine way to find us once. After that, the relationship belongs to the guest and the home. Lower rate, better service, repeat stays — everyone wins.

items.title: Boutique, never bland.
items.body: Every home has a personality. We don't make 134 copies of the same room. The trade-off is that we'll never run a thousand homes — and we're at peace with that.

philosophy.title: Why the language matters.
philosophy.body: The hospitality industry calls them units, listings, properties. We call them homes — because the difference shows up in the work. A home gets a real kitchen. A unit gets a kettle. A home gets a bookshelf. A unit gets a TV. The word changes what we look at, and what we look at changes what we ship.

items.title: First home in Lotus.
items.body: A 2BR we ran ourselves, on weekends, while keeping our day jobs.

items.title: Operations team.
items.body: Three full-time team members. First housekeeping playbook.

items.title: Golden Gates compound.
items.body: Our largest cluster opens — fifteen apartments and our first proper concierge desk.

items.title: The app.
items.body: Smart check-in goes live. 90% of arrivals now happen without a human handover.

items.title: Nomads.
items.body: A long-stay project for remote workers — co-working floor, monthly rates, slower stays.

items.title: Direct-first.
items.body: We launch this site to bring more guests into the direct relationship.

team.title: A small team that takes hosting personally.
team.subtitle: We hire slowly, train carefully, and stay small on purpose. Twelve full-time, plus a careful network of partners across cleaning, transport, and concierge.

careers.title: We hire one role at a time.
careers.body: If a Travelholic stay made you want to do this for a living, we want to hear from you. Send a note — even if no role is posted.

cancellation.title: Cancellation
cancellation.body: Free cancellation up to 7 days before check-in. Within 7 days, the first night is non-refundable. Within 48 hours, the booking is non-refundable. Long-stay terms differ — see the booking dialog for the exact policy.

experiences.closingEyebrow: Need something not on the list?
experiences.closingBody: If it can be done in Cairo, our concierge can usually find a way. Send a message — the team replies in minutes, not hours.
